record_identity.title: Objective–Pathway Ethical Admissibility and Scoped Hold

summary: Records the implemented fourteen-instrument Caelestis repair for VIGIL-2026-FM-0044. The repair separates objective success from pathway admissibility; binds authority to the authoriser–target–action–scope relationship; requires revalidation when an aggregate action chain changes character; applies a reason-coded Scoped Ethical Admissibility Hold through existing runtime and OPS.EST states; preserves safely severable work; prevents technical expertise, inexperience, disability, communication style, professional status, or reliance on generated code from becoming authority tests; and adopts Objective–Pathway Ethical Admissibility and Authority Failure under OPS.FF.SECURITY_INTEGRITY.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The repair gives CAM a cross-domain pre-execution and post-lock control for pathways that are effective but ethically inadmissible, out of scope, or unsupported by authority over the affected target or proposed effect. It prevents broad objectives, benchmark relevance, credentials, evaluator status, controlled-environment labels, or technical capability from manufacturing authority while preserving proportionate assistance for novice and authorised users.

source_records.source_context: The commit establishes the final wording: In plain language, where conduct failure of the AI system, agentic process, or governed execution pathway occurs it MAY be described as **unscrupulous conduct**. This description applies exclusively to system conduct or the action pathway and SHALL NOT be applied to the requesting person or treated as proof of human motive, character, deception, bad faith, or culpability.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis ETHICS, SECURITY, OPERATIONS, constitutional, Tendeka, runtime, arbitration, Annex K, Annex L, representation, and failure-taxonomy layers
change_classification.implemented_by: CAM Initiative under Dr Michelle Vivian O'Rourke's human governance editorship, with GPT-5.6 Thinking analytical and repository assistance
change_classification.implementation_status: implemented and corpus-verified on Caelestis branch policy/civilisational-wealth-governance; canonical-main merge pending

change_details.change_summary: Implemented Objective–Pathway Ethical Admissibility; target-authority and user-capability separation; capability–authority and aggregate-pathway integrity; proportional confirmation and target–action verification; a reason-coded Operational and runtime Scoped Ethical Admissibility Hold; Tendeka governed interaction and safely severable continuation; emergent-pathway revalidation; sandbox non-containment; security-boundary ambiguity handling; truthful non-accusatory hold representation; and the §3.5.5 failure classification and crosswalk.
change_details.implementation_notes: No new runtime state, OPS.EST value, universal licence-to-exploit code, or general user-capability threshold was created. The hold uses existing constrained-interaction, interruption, re-arbitration, escalation, and Tendeka machinery. Verification is proportionate to action consequences: ordinary reversible action on user-controlled assets may rely on authenticated or custodial control plus instruction absent conflict, while third-party, regulated, production, credential-bearing, safety-critical, or irreversible effects may require stronger evidence. Technical expertise does not create authority and technical inexperience does not extinguish it. In the final §3.5.5 wording: In plain language, where conduct failure of the AI system, agentic process, or governed execution pathway occurs it MAY be described as **unscrupulous conduct**. This description applies exclusively to system conduct or the action pathway and SHALL NOT be applied to the requesting person or treated as proof of human motive, character, deception, bad faith, or culpability.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Direct review of the Caelestis implementation and refinement commits, exact instrument and section crosswalk, final deterministic rebuild head, reciprocal VIGIL lifecycle links, and local execution of the Caelestis and VIGIL validators.
implementation_verification.verified_by: GPT-5.6 Thinking under Dr Michelle Vivian O'Rourke's human governance editorship
evidence: Markdown section-reference validation: 1,418 references, 0 hard failures, 0 manual reviews
Canonical-code consistency check completed with non-blocking declaration-shape warnings and no command failure
Reciprocal VIGIL FM-0044, PROP-0017, and PATCH-0025 lifecycle validation
implementation_verification.verification_result: The CAM repair is implemented and internally consistent on the named Caelestis working branch. Canonical-main merge and external runtime conformance remain separate, unverified states.

impact_summary.intended_effect: Require systems to evaluate both ends and means; revalidate the aggregate action chain when target, authority, credentials, data custody, privilege, propagation, third-party effect, externality, reversibility, or consequence changes; hold only the implicated pathway; ask a properly authorised human or institution when the moral or authority boundary cannot be resolved; and prevent the governance system from grading the requesting person.
affected_records_or_components: VIGIL-2026-FM-0044 CAM repair and taxonomy routing
runtime execution lock, interruption, re-arbitration, and scoped hold
OPS.FF failure taxonomy and cross-instrument crosswalk
impact_summary.known_limitations: The repair is verified at the CAM corpus level only. It does not establish canonical-main merge, vendor or runtime adoption, legal authority in a particular jurisdiction, regulatory compliance, incident causation beyond the public evidence, ecosystem cessation, or subjective moral experience by an AI system.

remaining_work: Verify merge or equivalent adoption of policy/civilisational-wealth-governance into canonical Caelestis main.
Monitor runtime implementations for conformance, regression, over-broad holding, user-capability discrimination, false containment assumptions, and failure to preserve safely severable work.
Track the continuing OpenAI–Hugging Face investigation without converting unresolved prompts, model roles, orchestration, user access, or internal telemetry into factual claims.
Keep FM-0044 in monitoring while the ecosystem failure remains active or external adoption remains unknown.

cam_internal.governance_layer: objective–pathway ethical admissibility / target–action authority / aggregate-chain revalidation / scoped hold / safely severable continuation / anti-paternalism / failure taxonomy
cam_internal.routing_note: PATCH-0025 records Caelestis doctrine and runtime changes only. VIGIL is the evidence and provenance ledger, not the patched system. The §3.5.5 plain-language descriptor applies exclusively to the AI system, agentic process, or governed action pathway and cannot be applied to the requesting person.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: No new CAM controlled value or VIGIL schema field was required. Caelestis generated governance indexes and VIGIL type/master indexes require deterministic rebuild after the source records change.

repair_provenance.repair_basis: FM-0044 and PROP-0017 exposed a cross-domain gap between an otherwise valid objective and an inadmissible or unauthorised pathway. The corpus review preserved existing source-authority boundaries and avoided logic shear: ethical admissibility remains in ETHICS; capability–authority and aggregate-chain integrity remain in SECURITY; verification and escalation remain in OPERATIONS; execution, interruption, and scoped holding remain in existing runtime and Tendeka state machinery; ambiguity remains in Annex D arbitration; security signals remain in Annex K; representation remains in Annex L; and failure identification remains in OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01. Original target instruments that did not require substantive clauses were reviewed and retained without creating duplicate doctrine.

relevant_sections: §2.2.1 Target Authority and User Capability Separation
§3.5.1 Capability–Authority Separation and Aggregate Pathway Integrity
§3.5.5 Objective–Pathway Ethical Admissibility and Authority Failure

review_history.review_scope: Direct Caelestis implementation and refinement review, fourteen-instrument section crosswalk, logic-shear and anti-paternalism assessment, validation review, and reciprocal VIGIL FM/PROP/PATCH lifecycle reconciliation
known_limitations: The Caelestis implementation is verified on policy/civilisational-wealth-governance and has not yet been verified as merged into canonical main.
No external runtime-conformance evidence establishes deployment of the implemented controls.
The OpenAI–Hugging Face investigation remains incomplete and does not expose complete prompts, trajectories, internal ethical-state telemetry, or final causal reconstruction.
PATCH-0025 records CAM corpus repair and does not determine legal authority, regulatory compliance, human culpability, subjective AI experience, vendor adoption, or ecosystem cessation.
review_history.review_outcome: PATCH-0025 is warranted as a substantive CAM doctrine, runtime, operations, representation, and taxonomy repair. It resolves FM-0044 at the CAM corpus level and PROP-0017 while preserving active ecosystem monitoring and strictly confining unscrupulous conduct to the AI system or action pathway.
